label: hs-CRP
summary_copy: Inflammation signal that helps frame recovery load and readiness context.
why_it_matters: Higher inflammation may reduce readiness, recovery quality, and tolerance for high-density training blocks.
monitoring_notes: Interpret longitudinally and compare against personal baselines rather than a single isolated draw.
load_relevance: Useful alongside workload, soreness, sleep, and force-plate changes after dense training or competition.
athlete_safe_copy: When this runs high, it is usually a signal to support recovery inputs first.
practitioner_copy: Contextualize against sample timing, recent contact load, illness, and recovery practices before escalating.
sample_notes: Avoid hard training for roughly two hours before collection.
Keep collection timing and posture as consistent as possible.

label: Vitamin D
summary_copy: Micronutrient support marker tied to recovery, immune support, and performance readiness context.
why_it_matters: Lower levels can align with slower recovery support and suboptimal readiness habits.
monitoring_notes: Use consistent testing windows and pair with nutrition, travel, and sun-exposure context.
load_relevance: Lower support can matter more during heavy blocks, travel periods, and low-sun months.
athlete_safe_copy: This helps guide food, supplementation, and recovery conversations.
practitioner_copy: Avoid one-size-fits-all recommendations; use repeat testing and broader clinical context.
sample_notes: Track against season timing and sun exposure.
Use the same lab cadence where possible.

label: Ferritin
summary_copy: Storage marker that can shape how recovery, energy, and training tolerance are interpreted.
why_it_matters: Values outside the intended range can influence fatigue interpretation and recovery planning.
monitoring_notes: Interpret with full context; ferritin is not a standalone fatigue explanation.
load_relevance: Helpful when energy, recovery, and training tolerance change across a season.
athlete_safe_copy: This marker helps explain recovery support needs, not identity or talent.
practitioner_copy: Review alongside full blood context and recent illness, inflammation, or supplementation changes.
sample_notes: Interpret with surrounding markers, not in isolation.

label: Cortisol
summary_copy: Context marker for stress load, recovery quality, and training-density conversations.
why_it_matters: Values can shift with travel, sleep loss, high stress, and high training density.
monitoring_notes: Time of day matters. Longitudinal interpretation is more useful than a single isolated result.
load_relevance: Relevant when training load and lifestyle stress both rise together.
athlete_safe_copy: This helps explain why sleep and stress-management habits matter.
practitioner_copy: Compare to collection timing, sleep, travel, and acute stress before making changes.
sample_notes: Interpret with sleep, travel, and stress context.
